Class Controller

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am25.server.model.Controller.Controller
public class Controller extends Object
MVC controller for a Mesos game session. Validates player actions against the current game phase and turn, delegates them to the Game model, and handles all phase transitions (placing → resolve-action → next round → end game).

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Controller()
    Creates the MVC controller for a Mesos game session.

    Returns all players currently in the game.

    Returns true if the game has reached the GAME_PHASE.END_GAME phase.

    Registers a ServerVirtualView as an observer of the game model, so the client associated with that view receives all game-event notifications (phase changes, market updates, board updates, etc.).

    Loads a saved game from disk and restores the game state.

    Handles a player disconnection at the game-logic level.
    void
    notifyPlayerReconnected(String nickname)
    Marks a reconnected player as connected again and re-adds them to the end of the turn queues so they participate from the next turn/round.

    Places the player's totem on the offer tile at the given board position during the placing phase.

    Handles the action of a player attempting to pass or skip their turn.
    void

    Registers the reconnection of a player to a loaded game, marking them as connected.
    void
    resumeGame()
    Pushes the full game state (market, board, era, phase, current turn) to all registered observers.

    Lets the player pick a tribe member or building from the previous-round (bottom) market row.
    void
    selectCardFromTopList(Player player, CARD_TYPE cardType, int position)
    Lets the player pick a tribe member or building from the current-round (top) market row.
    void
    selectExtraCard(Player player, CARD_TYPE cardType, int position)
    Resolves the bonus draw granted by the draw-one-more mechanic: picks the chosen card from the top market row and adds it to the player's tribe.
    void
    skipExtraDraw(Player player)
    Called when the player declines the extra draw granted by the draw-one-more building effect.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Controller

      public Controller()
      Creates the MVC controller for a Mesos game session. The Game instance is not created here — call createGame(Player, int) first.

  • Method Details

    • createGame

      public void createGame(Player playerHost, int playerNumber) throws IllegalStateException
      Initializes the Game model with the host player and the required player count. Must be called exactly once before any other game logic.
      Parameters:
      playerHost - the host player, added as the first participant.
      playerNumber - the total number of players required to start the game (2–5).
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if a game has already been created on this controller.

    • linkObserver

      public void linkObserver(ServerVirtualView virtualView)
      Registers a ServerVirtualView as an observer of the game model, so the client associated with that view receives all game-event notifications (phase changes, market updates, board updates, etc.).
      Parameters:
      virtualView - the virtual view to register.

    • getAllPlayers

      public List<Player> getAllPlayers()
      Returns all players currently in the game.
      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of Player instances.

    • crossRegisterPlayerObservers

      public void crossRegisterPlayerObservers(List<ServerVirtualView> views)
      Cross-registers every provided ServerVirtualView as a PlayerObserver on every player.

      By default each Player only notifies its own view when its tribe changes. Calling this method after all players have been created ensures that every client receives the addedCardToTribe notification whenever any player draws a card, so all clients can display an up-to-date view of the other players' tribes.

      Parameters:
      views - the list of all connected ServerVirtualView instances.

    • addPlayer

      public void addPlayer(Player player)
      Adds a player to the game lobby. If the lobby is already full (game not in SETUP phase) the call is silently ignored. When the added player fills the lobby, the game starts automatically and transitions to the PLACING_PHASE.
      Parameters:
      player - player to add

    • controllerGameStar

      public void controllerGameStar()
      Starts the Mesos game and pushes the initial state snapshot to all connected clients. Must be called after all players have joined and all observers have been linked.

    • placingPlayer

      public void placingPlayer(Player playerToPlace, int position) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException, TileOccupiedException
      Places the player's totem on the offer tile at the given board position during the placing phase. The chosen tile determines how many draws from the top and bottom market rows the player will have in the resolve-action phase. When all players have placed their totems the game automatically advances to the RESOLVE_ACTION phase.
      Parameters:
      playerToPlace - the player placing their totem.
      position - the index of the target offer tile on the board.
      Throws:
      ActionNotAvailable - if the game is not in a placing phase or it is not this player's turn to place.
      TileOccupiedException - if another player's totem is already on that tile.
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if position is out of range.

    • selectCardFromTopList

      public void selectCardFromTopList(Player player, CARD_TYPE cardType, int position) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException, NotEnoughFoodException, NotSelectableCardException, EmptyMarketException
      Lets the player pick a tribe member or building from the current-round (top) market row. When the player exhausts all remaining actions, the turn automatically advances to the next player. If no more players remain in this round, the round is advanced via Game.nextRoundIter().
      Parameters:
      player - the player who will receive the selected card.
      cardType - the type of card to be selected (tribe member or CARD_TYPE.BUILDING).
      position - the index of the card within the top row.
      Throws:
      ActionNotAvailable - if the game is not in a resolve-action phase, it is not this player's turn, or their offer tile grants no top-row draws.
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if position is out of range.
      NotEnoughFoodException - if the player lacks the food to buy a building.
      NotSelectableCardException - if the card at that position is an event card.
      EmptyMarketException - if the top row has no selectable cards.

    • selectCardFromBottomList

      public void selectCardFromBottomList(Player player, CARD_TYPE cardType, int position) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException, NotEnoughFoodException, NotSelectableCardException, EmptyMarketException
      Lets the player pick a tribe member or building from the previous-round (bottom) market row. When the player exhausts all remaining actions, the turn automatically advances to the next player. If no more players remain in this round, the round is advanced via Game.nextRoundIter().
      Parameters:
      player - the player who will receive the selected card.
      cardType - the type of card to be selected (tribe member or CARD_TYPE.BUILDING).
      position - the index of the card within the bottom row.
      Throws:
      ActionNotAvailable - if the game is not in a resolve-action phase, it is not this player's turn, or their offer tile grants no bottom-row draws.
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if position is out of range.
      NotEnoughFoodException - if the player lacks the food to buy a building.
      NotSelectableCardException - if the card at that position is an event card.
      EmptyMarketException - if the bottom row has no selectable cards.

    • playerDoNothing

      public void playerDoNothing(Player player) throws Exception
      Handles the action of a player attempting to pass or skip their turn. This method can only be executed during the action resolution phases (RESOLVE_ACTION or LAST_ROUND_RESOLVE_ACTION) and only if it is the given player's turn.

      A player is strictly allowed to "do nothing" ONLY IF they have no legal moves left to play. If the player attempts to skip while they still have available actions on the board/market, the move is rejected and an exception is thrown. If the skip is valid, the turn automatically passes to the next player, or advances the game to the next round if the current playing phase is over.

      Parameters:
      player - The player attempting to skip their action phase.
      Throws:
      Exception - If the player attempts to pass but still has playable actions available.

    • selectExtraCard

      public void selectExtraCard(Player player, CARD_TYPE cardType, int position) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException, NotEnoughFoodException, NotSelectableCardException, EmptyMarketException
      Resolves the bonus draw granted by the draw-one-more mechanic: picks the chosen card from the top market row and adds it to the player's tribe.
      Parameters:
      player - the player claiming the extra card.
      cardType - the type of card being selected (tribe member or CARD_TYPE.BUILDING).
      position - the index of the card in the top market row.
      Throws:
      NotEnoughFoodException - if the player lacks the food to buy a building.
      NotSelectableCardException - if the selected card is an event card.
      EmptyMarketException - if the top row has no selectable cards.
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if position is out of range.

    • skipExtraDraw

      public void skipExtraDraw(Player player)
      Called when the player declines the extra draw granted by the draw-one-more building effect. This is intentionally a no-op: askExtraDraw is sent fire-and-forget by the server, which does not block waiting for the player's response. The game has already advanced by the time this method is called, so no further action is needed.
      Parameters:
      player - the player declining the extra draw.

    • notifyPlayerDisconnected

      public void notifyPlayerDisconnected(String nickname)
      Handles a player disconnection at the game-logic level.
      1. Marks the player as CONNECTION_STATUS.DISCONNECTED in the model.
      2. Removes them from the placing/playing turn queues.
      3. Ends the game immediately if only one (or zero) connected players remain.
      4. If it was the disconnected player's turn, automatically advances to the next player.
      Parameters:
      nickname - the nickname of the disconnected player.

    • isGameOver

      public boolean isGameOver()
      Returns true if the game has reached the GAME_PHASE.END_GAME phase.
      Returns:
      true if the game is over, false otherwise.

    • forceEndGame

      public void forceEndGame()
      Forces the game to end immediately, running end-game scoring and notifying all clients. Called when too few connected players remain to continue.

    • notifyPlayerReconnected

      public void notifyPlayerReconnected(String nickname)
      Marks a reconnected player as connected again and re-adds them to the end of the turn queues so they participate from the next turn/round.
      Parameters:
      nickname - the nickname of the reconnected player.

    • loadGame

      public void loadGame(Player player) throws IllegalStateException, GameAlreadyLoadedException, NoGameToLoadException
      Loads a saved game from disk and restores the game state. Players are recreated from the persisted memento; the requesting player is marked as connected while all others are marked as disconnected, waiting to reconnect.
      Parameters:
      player - the player requesting the game load.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the game has already been initialised, or if the player's nickname is not present in the saved game.
      GameAlreadyLoadedException - if an active game instance already exists.
      NoGameToLoadException - if no save file exists.

    • reconnectLoadedPlayer

      public void reconnectLoadedPlayer(Player player) throws IllegalStateException, GameReadyToStartException
      Registers the reconnection of a player to a loaded game, marking them as connected. If all players are connected again, throws GameReadyToStartException to signal that the game can resume.
      Parameters:
      player - the player who is reconnecting.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the game has not been loaded yet.
      GameReadyToStartException - if all players are now connected and the game is ready to resume.

    • resumeGame

      public void resumeGame()
      Pushes the full game state (market, board, era, phase, current turn) to all registered observers. Called when resuming a loaded game after all players have reconnected, so every client gets an up-to-date snapshot.
